title: Ankylobus hamosus
taxonomy: Ankylobus hamosus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Madagascar, a country known for its unique and diverse plant species. This plant is commonly referred to as a climbing shrub, utilizing its long stems to grow upwards and support itself. The classification of Ankylobus hamosus is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Gentianales. This specific classification is based on its characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family.
uses: Ankylobus hamosus, also known as the club-shaped succulent, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
